title: Voluntary AI Safety Standard
notes: Browser-verified against the official DISR/NAIC digital publication on 19 July 2026. The landing page identifies the publisher as the National Artificial Intelligence Centre, dates publication to 5 September 2024, describes 10 voluntary guardrails across the AI supply chain, and states that the Guidance for AI Adoption published on 21 October 2025 is updated and simplified guidance that evolves the VAISS. The legal-landscape page confirms the standard creates no new legal obligations. The former official PDF URL now returns 404 and the live page exposes no downloadable file, so this review deliberately fingerprints the current official HTML publication without retaining a dead document link. Staging only; publication remains subject to Daniel Alkurdi review and explicit approval.

proposedRecord.title: Voluntary AI Safety Standard
proposedRecord.description: Australia's first voluntary AI safety standard, published in September 2024 with 10 governance guardrails and updated in October 2025 through the Guidance for AI Adoption.
proposedRecord.content: Published on 5 September 2024, Australia's first Voluntary AI Safety Standard set out 10 voluntary guardrails for organisations across the AI supply chain: accountability and governance; risk and impact management; data governance and security; testing and monitoring; human oversight; user transparency; contestability; supply-chain transparency; compliance records; and stakeholder engagement, fairness and inclusion. The standard did not create new legal obligations; it was designed to help organisations comply with existing Australian law, emerging guidance and community expectations. On 21 October 2025, the National AI Centre published the Guidance for AI Adoption, updating and simplifying the standard into 6 essential practices. This historical record is linked as superseded by that current guidance.
proposedRecord.aiSummary: Historical 2024 voluntary standard of 10 AI governance guardrails, evolved into the Guidance for AI Adoption in October 2025.

proposedRecord.title: National AI Plan
proposedRecord.description: Whole-of-government strategic framework for growing Australia’s AI industry and guiding economy-wide AI adoption, capability, investment and safety.
agencies: Department of Industry, Science and Resources
proposedRecord.content: Published on 2 December 2025, the National AI Plan sets Australian Government direction through three goals and nine actions: capture opportunities through smart infrastructure, domestic AI capability and investment; spread benefits through adoption, workforce skills and public services; and keep Australians safe through harm mitigation, responsible practices and global norms. It is a strategic framework rather than legislation. Implementation is iterative and evidence-based, supported by existing technology-neutral laws and sector regulators, and includes establishing the Australian AI Safety Institute.
proposedRecord.aiSummary: Whole-of-government framework with three goals and nine actions for AI industry growth, broad adoption and safety.

source.title: National AI Plan | Department of Industry Science and Resources

proposedRecord.title: AI Plan for the Australian Public Service 2025
proposedRecord.description: Australian Government plan for substantially increasing safe and responsible AI adoption across the APS through governance, workforce capability and secure tools.
proposedRecord.content: Published in November 2025, the APS AI Plan is limited to AI adoption and use by Australian Government agencies rather than industry or personal use. It organises APS action around three pillars—Trust, People and Tools—and sets measures including foundational AI training, access to secure AI tools, Chief AI Officers, and tracking and reporting of AI use. The plan is jointly owned by the Department of Finance, Digital Transformation Agency and Australian Public Service Commission.
proposedRecord.aiSummary: APS-wide AI adoption plan built around Trust, People and Tools, published in November 2025 and jointly owned by Finance, DTA and APSC.

source.title: AI Plan for the Australian Public Service 2025 | digital.gov.au

proposedRecord.title: Artificial intelligence | 2025 Implementation Plan
proposedRecord.description: Artificial intelligence chapter of the third annual Data and Digital Government Strategy Implementation Plan, covering APS AI adoption, governance, capability and delivery priorities.
proposedRecord.content: The third annual Data and Digital Government Strategy Implementation Plan identifies artificial intelligence as one of four priorities for the coming year. Its AI chapter covers adoption across the Australian Public Service, responsible-use policy and governance, public- and private-sector use cases, productivity, and workforce capability. The official version 1.0 PDF says the plan was first published in November 2025, while the plan landing page was last updated on 17 November 2025. Policai therefore records the publication date at month precision rather than inventing a day.

analysis.summary: Whole-of-government policy setting mandatory requirements for the safe, transparent, and accountable use of AI by Australian Government agencies.
agencies: Data and Digital Government Strategy Branch

sourceEvidence.title: Policy for the responsible use of AI in government - Version 2.0 | digital.gov.au

browserCapture.notes: Captured from the official digital.gov.au policy page in Google Chrome after Policai's hardened retriever timed out; the linked version 2.0 PDF was downloaded through the same official page and its bytes are fingerprinted by the MCP server.

reviewedDate.notes: Confirmed the version 2.0 effective date of 15 December 2025 on the official digital.gov.au policy page and in the linked official PDF.

proposedRecord.title: Policy for the Responsible Use of AI in Government
proposedRecord.description: Commonwealth policy setting mandatory requirements for the safe, transparent, and accountable use of AI by non-corporate Commonwealth entities.
proposedRecord.content: Version 2.0 took effect on 15 December 2025 and applies to all non-corporate Commonwealth entities, with specified exceptions. It requires accountable officials, transparency statements, a strategic approach to AI adoption, operational controls for responsible AI use, AI use-case accountability and registers, staff training, and AI use-case impact assessments.
proposedRecord.aiSummary: Core Commonwealth policy governing responsible AI use by non-corporate Commonwealth entities from 15 December 2025.

title: Agentic AI addendum to the AI technical standard for Australian Government
notes: Discovered during the scheduled manual review of the digital.gov.au AI policy hub.

analysis.summary: DTA addendum extending the Australian Government AI technical standard with secure and governed agentic-AI implementation guidance.

proposedRecord.title: Agentic AI addendum to the AI technical standard for Australian Government
proposedRecord.description: Australian Government best-practice standard for secure and governed implementation of agentic AI systems.
proposedRecord.content: The Agentic AI addendum provides best-practice guidance for Australian Government agencies exploring, developing or using agentic AI. It applies alongside the Australian Government AI technical standard and addresses agentic considerations across the AI lifecycle, including design, data, training, evaluation, integration, monitoring and decommissioning. The underlying technical-standard statements, criteria and general guidance continue to apply.
proposedRecord.aiSummary: DTA addendum extending the Australian Government AI technical standard with secure and governed agentic-AI implementation guidance.

verification.notes: Checked against the official DTA page, including scope and the displayed 4 June 2026 last-updated date. Migration review on 17 July 2026 confirmed that the page remained available through an independent browser retrieval, but Policai's controlled retriever timed out and no canonical fingerprint could be established. The record is withheld pending fingerprinted re-verification.

approvalNotes: Checked against the official DTA page, including scope and the displayed 4 June 2026 last-updated date.
